title: rubus hybridus subsp. pygmaeus
intro: Rubus hybridus subsp. pygmaeus, also known as the dwarf raspberry or alpine raspberry, is a rare and small shrub native to high-altitude regions in Europe. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of anthocyanins, which are powerful antioxidants that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus hybridus subsp. pygmaeus include its potential to reduce oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Rubus hybridus subsp. pygmaeus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
